# Release v1.2.0

## Summary

`v1.2.0` expands the OpenClaw Event Server Plugin from the older
session/tool-centric hook surface into the full modern run lifecycle. The
plugin now emits canonical events for model resolution, prompt construction,
model input/output, agent completion, and session compaction, while defaulting
those new payloads to metadata-only transport so operators do not accidentally
ship raw prompt and transcript content downstream.

This release also hardens the local release process itself. The repository now
has an explicit Node 20 release-lane verification command that refreshes the
dependency tree with `npm ci` and mirrors the real publish environment before a
tag is created.

## Highlights

- Added canonical event coverage for:
  - `agent.before_model_resolve`
  - `agent.before_prompt_build`
  - `agent.llm_input`
  - `agent.llm_output`
  - `agent.end`
  - `session.before_compaction`
  - `session.after_compaction`
- Added `privacy.payloadMode=metadata|full` with `metadata` as the default for
  the new modern lifecycle hooks.
- Added derived lifecycle audit fields on `agent.llm_input` so downstream
  systems can detect prompt/history changes without requiring raw prompt bodies.
- Added a shared Node 20 release-lane preflight:
  - `npm run verify:release-lane`

## Modern Lifecycle Coverage

The plugin now maps the modern OpenClaw typed hook surface directly into stable
canonical events:

- `before_model_resolve -> agent.before_model_resolve`
- `before_prompt_build -> agent.before_prompt_build`
- `llm_input -> agent.llm_input`
- `llm_output -> agent.llm_output`
- `agent_end -> agent.end`
- `before_compaction -> session.before_compaction`
- `after_compaction -> session.after_compaction`

This matters because these are stable plugin hook surfaces, not incidental
runtime debug lines. Downstream consumers can now observe the actual OpenClaw
agent-run lifecycle directly instead of inferring behavior from embedded-run log
messages.

Compaction was intentionally modeled as `session.*` rather than `agent.*`
because the compacted object is the session transcript itself. The acting
`agentId` is still preserved when available so event consumers can query both
the subject and the actor cleanly.

## Privacy And Payload Shaping

The new lifecycle hooks can expose raw prompt text, transcript messages, and
assistant responses. Shipping that content by default would have widened the
plugin’s data-retention footprint substantially, so this release introduces a
separate privacy control:

- `privacy.payloadMode: "metadata"` (default)
- `privacy.payloadMode: "full"`

In metadata mode, the lifecycle events still carry useful operational context:

- prompt lengths
- message counts
- message-role summaries
- assistant text counts and lengths
- provider/model identifiers
- compaction counters
- derived prompt/history deltas

In full mode, the upstream payloads are preserved for operators who explicitly
want them for tightly controlled debugging or observability workflows.

## Hook Bridge And Tool Guard Scope

Hook Bridge now sees the new lifecycle events through the same canonical event
path as the rest of the plugin. That means normal rule matching works across:

- `eventType`
- `agentId`
- `sessionId`
- `sessionKey`
- `data.*` selectors

Tool Guard does **not** run for these hooks. That is intentional. Tool Guard is
still a synchronous `before_tool_call` enforcement mechanism, not a general
policy engine for prompt/model hooks. The docs now make that scope boundary
explicit so operators do not mistake observability coverage for enforcement
coverage.

## Runtime And Correctness Improvements

This release adds a short-lived tracker for the modern run phases so
`agent.llm_input` can report how the eventual model request differs from earlier
hook phases. That supports downstream auditing use cases like:

- detecting prompt changes after `before_prompt_build`
- measuring prompt growth between `before_model_resolve` and `llm_input`
- spotting history-count deltas introduced before model invocation

During release review we found and fixed a subtle edge case in that tracker:
when OpenClaw omitted an upstream `runId`, the tracker could drift onto a
synthetic fallback key and lose correlation with the earlier lifecycle phase
snapshots. The implementation now keeps `runId` optional so tracker correlation
stays anchored to session identity when needed.

## Release Engineering

This release also improves the local publishing workflow:

- Added `scripts/release-node-env.sh` so the release flow and manual preflight
  share the same Node/npm resolution logic.
- Added `npm run verify:release-lane`, which:
  - switches to the pinned release toolchain
  - runs `npm ci`
  - executes the same verification command used by the publish lane
- Updated release documentation so local release prep matches the actual
  GitHub-based publishing flow.
- Corrected compatibility references to the current pinned OpenClaw hook
  surface fixture.

## Fixes

- Fixed modern lifecycle audit tracking when `runId` is absent but session
  identity exists.
- Corrected stale compatibility references in public docs.
- Corrected local agent guidance so it reflects the new metadata-first privacy
  default instead of implying raw lifecycle payloads are always emitted.

## Breaking Changes

- None.

## Upgrade Notes

- No migration is required for existing installs.
- The new lifecycle events are available immediately after upgrade.
- If you want raw prompt/model/transcript content for those new hooks, opt in
  explicitly with:
  - config: `privacy.payloadMode: "full"`
  - env: `EVENT_PLUGIN_MODERN_LIFECYCLE_PAYLOAD_MODE=full`
- If you do nothing, the new lifecycle hooks emit metadata-only payloads by
  default.

## Validation

Release validation was run on the repo-pinned release lane:

- `npm run verify:release-lane`

Result:

- Node `v20.20.0`
- 43 passing test suites
- 285 passing tests
- coverage thresholds satisfied
